lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Miaoww

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Bob!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Hmm... Wish I had a cat to talk to.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Erm, hello? Can you understand me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I certainly can. I am wearing this amulet I got in Sophanem!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Ah so you've met some of the local felines. Wise and confusing, those Sphinges.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow!! You know the plural of Sphinx!! What else do you know?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: All sorts of stuff! I pick up no end of comments on my travels. Want to hear one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I certainly do. Share your wisdom Bob!

sections.title: Alternative dialogue while wearing a catspeak amulet

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Bob, how's tricks?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Tricks? Dogs are for tricks! Cats are for admiring and fussing over.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: OK then, how about a joke instead?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Woof woof. Yip yip!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Ah, don't mind me. I am learning a second language!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, you don't understand. I wanted to hear a joke.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Sigh... OK. Did you hear about the cat who drank 10 bowls of milk? He set a new lap record.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did he? Wow. What about this joke then?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Are you trying to be funny?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, that's your job isn't it?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Grrrowl. OK, last try. What do you call a cat that lives in an igloo?.... An Eskimew!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: An Eskimew... That's not funny. I waited all this time for that? Sheesh.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: You know, sometimes I wonder why I still bother with this place. It's just not the same since the humans arrived.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Speak to me, Bob! Give me some wise words of advice!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Advice you want eh? Well, I could just enlighten you with a snippet of my vast knowledge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That would be fantastic! I heard you were very very wise indeed.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Well, I think I will let you decide that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: OK, bamboozle me with your intelligence!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Hi, my name is Bob.

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: Hello Bob!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you two know each other?

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: I don't think so...

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Yes you do. Dive deep, search your feelings.

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: OK, I will try.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's up puss?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: If only you knew the power of Bob. Your master never told you who your real father was, did he?

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: No, but what's that got to do with... no, nooooooo! It cannot be!!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Yes. I am your father!

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: That's impossible!!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: You can destroy Zamorak. He has foreseen this. It is your destiny. Join me, and together we can rule this place as father and son.

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: OK dad, maybe later, you're scaring me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think that's about enough of that. I'm off.

sections.title: If the player has a cat in the bank

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: [player name], why do you keep your cat in the bank?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought it was too dangerous to bring it out with me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Or, I could let the poor thing get butchered! Is that what you want?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Butchered!!! I think not. Cats are easily as tough as sheep. Cats were the second thing Guthix brought here, after sheep of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Weird. I thought Gnomes and the Dwarves were the first ones. Oh well back to school for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Bob. I didn’t think I would ever see you round here. Long way from home isn’t it?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Yes I guess so. But I’ve been asked to get some coal for Dunstan. He ran out, you see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything I can do to help?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: No thanks. I’m on my way back now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok Bob, see you around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Bob. I didn't think I would ever see you round here. Long way from home isn't it?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Yes, but the fish round here are oh so tasty! And there's nobody who can make a good old fashioned seafood gumbo like the Lumbridge cook!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gumbo?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Oh yes, an old recipe passed down from generation to generation of cooks. But this might be the last generation since the Duke's current cook doesn't have any children.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, I will have to ask him for the recipe then.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Oh, I wouldn't do that if I were you, he's very sensitive about his gumbo responsibilities. I'm sure that if you're a good enough fisherman and cook the knowledge will reach you one day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: OK, I shan't bring it up with him. Cheers for the chat Bob.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: No problem. Just make sure you bring me some of you ever get the chance to make it. I love that gumbo!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Bob. What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Ah, another person who thinks I want to talk to them just because they can understand me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aww, come on!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: OK, you asked what I'm doing here.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: I'm here to study the druids. They're boring, but they know how to treat animals, and they don't go around bothering you just because they've got a silly gadget that lets them understand what you say!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Bob. What are you doing hanging around Varrock?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Not much. Just came to see how things are going. Not visited here in a while you see. Me and Roald used to talk all the time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh really, about what?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Well, those white knights are far more complicated than you might imagine. Not the ‘goodie goodies’ they claim to be. Just watch your back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do Bob, and thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Bob. I didn't think I would ever see you round here. Long way from home isn't it?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Yikes!! Where did you pop up from?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh sorry Bob, didn't mean to make you jump.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Noooooo. Now that mouse I was chasing has got away. Can you be a bit more sneaky next time? You know, sneaky sneaky stick da chompy as my old friend Rantz often says.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure thing Bob.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Bob. I didn't think I would ever see you round here. Long way from home isn't it?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Argh! My thumbs have gone weird!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But Bob, you don't have any thumbs!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Exactly!! Clearly I must go home, the sky's beginning to bruise, night must fall and I shall be forced to camp.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is everything Ok Bob?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: No, not really. I've just had a severe disappointment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Whats up Bob, lost your favorite toy?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: No, it's just that there comes a time in every cats life where he realises how much time he has wasted just.... playing with rats.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Doesn't sound like a waste of time to me! They deserve it!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Ahh, I guess your right. Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Bob. I didn't think I would ever see you round here. Long way from home isn't it?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Actually no. I come from round here originally. Back then all this was just fields.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Back then? How old are you Bob?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Ha! How old indeed. How old are the trees? How old are the waves that crash against the shore? How old is the stone we walk on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Cryptic as ever. Cheers Bob.... I think.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: If a player falls down in a forest and nobody is there, do they make any noise?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Now, where did the rest of my PK clan go?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Be wery, wery quiet... I'm hunting pwayers.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Ah, don't mind me. I'm just looting bodies.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Look out!! He's over there!! Run!!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: You know, my feet are knackered. This walking lark isn't all it's cracked up to be. Give me a nice monitor to sleep on any day.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: In this place it's not HOW you die that matters. It's who you take with you.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Those who live by the sword, die by the mage.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Did you see that? Someone with a big shiny new halberd hiding behind that rock. I thought only rangers acted as cowardly as that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you really just a cat Bob?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Apparently so yes. Well, that's what they told me after the experiment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What experiment?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Yes. There was this professor called 'Oddenstein' I think. He must have tricked me into that machine of his, then the next thing I knew I was feline!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oddenstein? I am sure I have heard that name...

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Yes. Once the authorities found out about his experiments he ran away. Lucky for him or I would scratch his eyes out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is this really all true? Sounds a bit suspicious to me.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Never question the logic of a cat, however flawed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Flawed? So it isn't true... so, erm, eh?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Perhaps one day you will learn the truth. Until then, the Oddenstein story is what we will tell the 'others'. Understood?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: OK. Your secrets safe with me Bob.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is a long way from Burthorpe. What you doing out here?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: I am drawing a picture of that thing over there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? What thing?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: That gullible thing over there. Next to that tree where that man sometimes stands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow. Could you be any more vague and sarcastic?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Probably. It's just no challenge these days.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not safe here Bob. You should leave.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: No way. I am here catching mice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But there aren't any mice in the Wilderness.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Ahh, the human shows signs of a brain. Why do you think that is?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Because you caught them all?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Exactly. Well done!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Bob. Got any wisdom to share?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: If a player falls down in a forest and nobody is there, do they make any noise?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Sorry, I am far too busy marking my territory.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your territory? This place is ruled by the Grand Highmage Gower and his minions.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: I think not. They are my minions, and I let him think that he's in charge. I'm the real power behind the throne.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I knew it!!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Yeah, it keeps them happy that way, but don't tell anyone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hee hee. OK Bob.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Dogs believe they are human. Cats believe they are Guthix!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you believe, Bob?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Well, I don't believe much really since I know I am Zar... erm, well, no, I erm, meow?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hee hee. OK Bob.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Don't get distracted so easily! See, I'm distracting you right now!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Sorry, I'm far too busy calculating thermal vectors.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Eh?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Oh, sorry... I meant, erm, meow?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Nothing interesting happens.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Eh? That's hardly useful.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Ahh, but it is you see. It does all sorts of wonderous things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Like what?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Well, it's more to do with what happens when you hear that being said. You see, something interesting happens somewhere else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what you're saying is that if nothing interesting happens to me, then something interesting is happening for someone else somewhere that IS interesting?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Yes, that's about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: OK... I see.... I think.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Cats are rather delicate creatures and they are subject to a good many ailments, but I never heard of one who suffered from insomnia.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: So, you want to know how us cats keep so clean all day?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Must be all that washing you do.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: You would think so wouldn't you. But is being covered in spit really clean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Eeeww. Gross.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Anything not nailed down is a cat toy.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: If at first you don't succeed, take a short nap.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Yeah, I could tell you something really useful, but then I would have to '1-hit you' to death.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bob, you couldn't 1-hit a noob.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Care to place a wager on that? Sorry, hang on, I've got no pockets. Maybe next time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be waiting.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Dogs have owners, cats have staff.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: I have studied many philosophers and many cats. The wisdom of cats is infinitely superior.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: There are many intelligent species in the universe. They are all owned by cats.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Cats and teleporting just don't mix well. That's why I walk everywhere. Messes my hair up terribly.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Cats are smarter than dogs. You can't get eight cats to pull a sled through snow.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Sorry, no time for chat. Still got mouse bones stuck in my teeth. How awkward.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: You know, cats are the first species to fully evolve. We're perfect.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Preserve nature. Pickle a dog!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Do you like dogs?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Me too. Care to swap recipes? I have a rather unique hotdog sauce.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bob, you're a bit messed up really aren't you.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: No, I just know what I like. Besides, it was a joke.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A joke? I doubt the dogs are laughing.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: What dogs? I have already rid this place of dogs. Have you ever seen one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes actually. I heard there are a few wild dogs about here somewhere.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Right!! Fellow cats, prepare for war!!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Mmm. Maybe another time.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Cat Rule number 1: Always act afraid when near dogs, especially if you're near a human. They will pick you up and fuss you, then shout at the dog. Most amusing!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Cat Rule number 2: When assisting a human at work, make sure you sit behind its right leg. You stand a much better chance of getting picked up and stroked. Also, it's very amusing

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Cat Rule number 3: If you ever make a mistake, which is rare and probably involves a dog, make sure you wash yourself immediately. This will give everyone the impression that

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: it was done on purpose.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Cat Rule number 4: Beware the bag monster. These things live in small bags and under newspapers, sometimes even hidden in shoes if they are very clever. They will be very small and

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: take on the same colour as the hiding place. Attack these things on sight - stop at nothing. If you see a bag or shoe you have not inspected recently, pounce!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you like all the monsters around here?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: No, not really. I'm actually signed up with all those HAM fanatics. Kill the whole lot of 'em!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a bit cruel isn't it?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Yeah, that's one of my best qualities. Just behind sneaky and vicious. Besides, after a few doses of catnip, they all look like mice to me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Bob! Do you have another one of those mouse toys? I lost mine.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Hi [player name]. Mouse toy?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, a fake mouse on a string.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Oh, you mean an ACTD.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Eh?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: An Advanced Combat Training Device, every cat should have one. Here's another one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have the space, one second.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Bob! You're the best!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: You're welcome!
